def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    
    window = params['window']
    rsi_window = params['rsi_window']
    rsi_lower = params['rsi_lower']
    rsi_upper = params['rsi_upper']
    
    # Calculate rolling support and resistance
    df['support'] = df['low'].rolling(window=window).min()
    df['resistance'] = df['high'].rolling(window=window).max()
    
    # Calculate RSI
    df['rsi'] = ta.momentum.rsi(df['close'], window=rsi_window)
    
    # Generate signals
    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index, dtype='int')
    
    # Long when price bounces off support with RSI oversold
    signals[(df['close'] > df['support']) & (df['close'].shift(1) <= df['support'].shift(1)) & (df['rsi'] < rsi_lower)] = 1
    
    # Short when price rejects resistance with RSI overbought
    signals[(df['close'] < df['resistance']) & (df['close'].shift(1) >= df['resistance'].shift(1)) & (df['rsi'] > rsi_upper)] = -1
    
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: AUD/USD exhibits strong support/resistance levels where price tends to reverse when approaching historical extremes, confirmed by RSI overbought/oversold conditions.
